content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed `origin/master...HEAD` (excluding `.ralph-burning/`), read the new propose-new-bead reconciliation paths in `service.rs`, `success_reconciliation.rs`, `final_review.rs`, and related tests, and found three correctness issues in the new follow-up-bead flow.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: `handle_propose_new_bead()` trusts `ProposedBeadCreated` journal entries and returns before querying `br`, so a follow-up bead that was later removed from `.beads` or lost its dependency on the active bead is treated as already created and never recreated or repaired. affected_files: ["src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs"] refs: src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2838-2848,2970-2975 severity: medium remediation_hint: Verify the journaled `created_bead_id` with `br show` (and repair the dependency if needed) before returning; if the bead is missing, fall back to the normal search/create path.
   Rationale: `.beads` is the authoritative backlog, but this path currently lets the milestone journal short-circuit the required defensive re-check and can silently drop accepted follow-up work after backlog drift or replay.
2. finding_summary: The defensive re-check reclassifies any open/in-progress/deferred bead with the same normalized title as planned-elsewhere, without checking `finding_summary`, `proposed_scope`, or `rationale`. A generic title collision will therefore suppress creation of genuinely new follow-up work and record a verified mapping to the wrong bead. affected_files: ["src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs"] refs: src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2978-2984,3034-3056 severity: high remediation_hint: Require a stronger semantic match before reclassifying (for example compare the rendered description/scope or another stable fingerprint), and only mark the mapping verified after that richer match succeeds.
   Rationale: This is a silent false-positive dedupe path: once it fires, the accepted propose-new-bead never reaches `.beads`, even though the existing bead may cover different work.
3. finding_summary: `reconcile_proposed_beads_after_success()` treats payload listing failures, aggregate parse failures, and missing propose-new-bead metadata as non-blocking and continues success reconciliation. Because propose-new-bead amendments are no longer queued anywhere else, those accepted findings are silently lost instead of keeping reconciliation open for retry. affected_files: ["src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs"] refs: src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:935-943,968-977,987-998 severity: medium remediation_hint: Once a final-review aggregate is expected for the run, bubble these cases up as `ReconciliationError::MilestoneUpdateFailed` so operators can retry rather than completing the bead with dropped follow-up work.
   Rationale: The new routing path has a single source of truth for propose-new-bead reconciliation; swallowing failures in that path permanently loses accepted follow-up work.
